methods.name: bluecat_libraries.address_manager.api.client.Client.replace_server
documentation:
Replace a server.

```
param server_id

The object ID of the server to replace.

param name

Name of the server to replace.

param default_interface

Management interface address for the server.

param host_name

The DNS FQDN by which the server is referenced.

param password

The server password. For more information on the default server password,
refer to BlueCat default login credentials.

param upgrade

Flag indicating that server needs to be upgraded or not. True means server
needs to be upgraded.

param properties

A dictionary containing the following options:

servicesIPv4Address - IPv4 address used only for services traffic such as DNS,
DHCP, DHCPv6 and TFTP. If dedicated management is enabled, this option must be
specified. If dedicated management is disabled, this address must be the same as
defaultInterfaceAddress which is management interface address.

servicesIPv4Netmask - IPv4 netmask used only for services traffic such as DNS,
DHCP, DHCPv6 and TFTP. If dedicated management is enabled, this option must be
specified. If dedicated management is disabled, this netmask address must be the same
as the management interface netmask address.

servicesIPv6Address - IPv6 address used only for services traffic such as DNS,
DHCP, DHCPv6 and TFTP. This is optional.

servicesIPv6Subnet - IPv6 subnet used only for services traffic such as DNS, DHCP,
DHCPv6 and TFTP. This is optional.

xhaIPv4Address - IPv4 address used for XHA. This is optional.

xhaIPv4Netmask - IPv4 netmask used for XHA. This is optional.

redundancyScenario - networking redundancy scenarios. The possible values are
ACTIVE_BACKUP Failover and IEEE_802_3AD Load Balancing.

resetServices - allow for replacing the DNS/DHCP Server while maintaining existing
configurations for DNS, DHCP, and TFTP services. Define this option only if the IPv4
or IPv6 addresses of the Services interface have been modified or the configurations
needs to be reset for DNS, DHCP, and TFTP services on the DNS/DHCP Server. The value
is either true or false by default, false.

encryptedNotification - A boolean value. It enables or disables encrypted
notifications between Address Manager and BlueCat DNS/DHCP Servers. This property is
optional and supported from Address Manager v9.4.0. The default value is false.

For DNS/DHCP Servers without multi-port support, the interface-related
property options will be ignored.

Resetting DNS/DHCP Servers will result in a service outage. This service
outage will last until services have been deployed to the replacement system. Only
reset DNS/DHCP Server services if the DNS/DHCP Server are being replaced with a new
appliance of a different type or reconfiguring the IPv4 or IPv6 addresses of the
Services interface on the appliance. BlueCat recommends that a maintenance window is
scheduled before performing a reset of DNS/DHCP Server services.
```

Example:

> ```python
> from bluecat_libraries.address_manager.api import Client
>
> server_id = <server_id>
> name = "server-name"
> default_interface = <ip_address>
> host_name = <existing_hostname>
> password = <server_password>
> upgrade = "false"
> properties = {
>     "servicesIPv4Address": <ip4_address>,
>     "servicesIPv4Netmask": <ipr_netmask>
> }
>
> with Client(<bam_host_url>) as client:
>     client.login(<username>, <password>)
>     client.replace_server(
>         server_id, name, default_interface, host_name, password, upgrade, properties
>     )
>     client.logout()
> ```

:::

methods.name: bluecat_libraries.address_manager.api.client.Client.add_dhcp_match_class
documentation:
Add a DHCP match class to Address Manager.

```
param configuration_id

The object ID of the configuration to which the DHCP match class is being added.

param name

The name of the DHCP match class.

param type

The type of the match criteria.
This type must be one of the constants listed for DHCP match class criteria.

param properties

Object properties, following properties and values:

description - A description of the match class.

matchOffset - The Match Offset value for the MatchClass.
It refers to the point where the match should begin.

matchLength - The Match Length value for the MatchClass. It refers to the number of characters to match.

customMatchRawString - A raw string that maps directly to a data or boolean expression for
DHCP_CLASS_CUSTOM_MATCH and DHCP_CLASS_CUSTOM_MATCH_IF constants.

matchOffset and matchLength only apply to the following five constants:

DHCP_CLASS_HARDWARE

DHCP_CLASS_CLIENT_ID

DHCP_CLASS_VENDOR_ID

DHCP_CLASS_AGENT_CIRCUIT_ID

DHCP_CLASS_AGENT_REMOTE_ID

matchOffset and matchLength must be specify together.

return

The object ID of the new DHCP match class.
```

Example:

> ```python
> from bluecat_libraries.address_manager.api import Client
> from bluecat_libraries.address_manager.constants import DHCPMatchClass
>
> configuration_id = <configuration_id>
> name = "dhcp-match-class-name"
> type = DHCPMatchClass.DHCP_CLASS_VENDOR_ID
> properties = {"matchOffset": 0, "matchLength": 256}
>
> with Client(<bam_host_url>) as client:
>     client.login(<username>, <password>)
>     entity_id = client.add_dhcp_match_class(configuration_id, name, type, properties)
>     client.logout()
> print(entity_id)
> ```

:::{versionadded} 21.8.1
:::
endregion Additional IP Addresses
region DHCP Match Class

description - a description of the match class.

The object ID of the new DHCP match class value.

methods.name: bluecat_libraries.address_manager.api.client.Client.add_parent_block
documentation:
Add a parent block. Create an IPv4 or IPv6 block from a list of IPv4 or IPv6 blocks or networks.
All blocks and networks must have the same parent but it does not need to be contiguous.

```
param object_ids

A list of the object IDs of IPv4 or IPv6 blocks or networks.

return

The object ID of the new IPv4 or IPv6 parent block.
This method does not create a name for the new block.
```

Example:

> ```python
> from bluecat_libraries.address_manager.api import Client
>
> object_ids = [<ip4_network1_id>,<ip4_network2_id>]
>
> with Client(<bam_host_url>) as client:
>     client.login(<username>, <password>)
>     ip4_block_id = client.add_parent_block(object_ids)
>     client.logout()
> print(ip4_block_id)
> ```

:::{versionadded} 21.8.1
:::

Add a parent block with properties. Create an IPv4 or IPv6 block with a name from a list of IPv4 or IPv6 blocks
or networks. All blocks and networks must have the same parent but it does not need to be contiguous.

methods.name: bluecat_libraries.address_manager.api.client.Client.merge_selected_blocks_or_networks
documentation:
Merge specified IPv4 blocks or IPv4 networks into a single IPv4 block or IPv4 network.
The list of objects to be merged must all be of the same type (for example, all blocks
or all networks). The objects must all have the same parent and must be contiguous.

```
param object_id_to_keep

The object ID of the IPv4 block or IPv4 network that will retain
its identity after the merge.

param object_ids_to_merge

The object IDs of the IPv4 blocks or IPv4 networks to be merged.
```

Example:

> ```python
> from bluecat_libraries.address_manager.api import Client
>
> object_ids_to_merge = [<ip4_block1_id>, <ip4_block2_id>, <ip4_block3_id>]
>
> with Client(<bam_host_url>) as client:
>     client.login(<username>, <password>)
>     client.merge_selected_blocks_or_networks(<ip4_block1_id>, object_ids_to_merge)
>     client.logout()
> ```

methods.name: bluecat_libraries.address_manager.api.client.Client.merge_blocks_with_parent
documentation:
Merge specified IPv4 blocks into a single block. The blocks must all have the same parent. If blocks are not
contiguous, The gap between blocks will be automatically found and made contiguous so they can be merged.
If the parent of the block is a configuration, they cannot contain networks.

```
param ip4_block_ids

The object IDs of the IPv4 blocks to be merged.
```

> from bluecat_libraries.address_manager.api import Client

>

methods.name: bluecat_libraries.address_manager.api.client.Client.add_ip4_reconciliation_policy
documentation:
Add an IPv4 reconciliation policy.

```
param parent_id

The object ID of the parent object of the policy.
The IPv4 reconciliation policies can be created at the configuration, IPv4 block, and
IPv4 network levels.

param name

The name of the IPv4 reconciliation policy.

param properties

Object properties and values listed in Reconciliation properties
and values.

The hour specified for  must be in 12-hour clock format.

return

The object ID of the new reconciliation policy.
```

Example:

> ```python
> from bluecat_libraries.address_manager.api import Client
> from bluecat_libraries.address_manager.constants import DiscoveryType, SNMPVersion
>
> parent_id = <configuration_id>
> name = "reconciliation-policy-name"
> properties = {
>     "discoveryType": DiscoveryType.SNMP,
>     "seedRouterAddress": "10.244.140.124",
>     "snmpVersion": SNMPVersion.V1,
>     "snmpPortNumber": 161,
>     "snmpCommunityString": "public",
>     "networkBoundaries": "10.0.0.0/8",
>     "schedule": "09:59AM,01 Nov 2021,ONCE",
> }
>
> with Client(<bam_host_url>) as client:
>     client.login(<username>, <password>)
>     policy_id = client.add_ip4_reconciliation_policy(parent_id, name, properties)
>     client.logout()
> print(policy_id)
> ```

:::{versionadded} 21.11.1
:::
endregion IPv4 Blocks

methods.name: bluecat_libraries.address_manager.api.client.Client.add_tftp_file
documentation:
Add a TFTP file.

```
param entity_id

The object ID of the parent object of the TFTP file. The parent can be a TFTP folder or
TFTP group.

param name

The name of the TFTP file.

param data

The data to be uploaded and distributed to clients by TFTP.

param version

The version of the file.

param properties

Object properties, including user-defined fields and description properties.

return

The object ID of the new TFTP file.
```

Example:

> ```python
> from bluecat_libraries.address_manager.api import Client
>
> name = "tftp-file-name"
> version = <file_version>
> properties = {<udf_name>: <udf_value>}
>
> # Add TFTP file where input data is a string
> with Client(<bam_host_url>) as client:
>     client.login(<username>, <password>)
>     tftp_file_id = client.add_tftp_file(<entity_id>, name, "example data", version, properties)
>     client.logout()
>
> # Add TFTP file where input data is an I/O stream
> with Client(<bam_host_url>) as client, open("test.png", "rb") as file:
>     client.login(<username>, <password>)
>     tftp_file_id = client.add_tftp_file(<entity_id>, name, file, version, properties)
>     client.logout()
> print(tftp_file_id)
> ```

:::{versionadded} 21.11.1
:::
endregion IPv4 Discovery and Reconciliation

methods.name: bluecat_libraries.address_manager.api.client.Client.add_tftp_deployment_role
documentation:
Add a TFTP deployment role to a specified object.

```
param tftp_group_id

The object ID of the TFTP group to which the TFTP deployment role is
being added.

param server_id

The object ID of the server interface with which the TFTP deployment role
associates.

param properties

Object properties, including user-defined fields.

The object ID of the new TFTP deployment role.
```

Example:

> ```python
> from bluecat_libraries.address_manager.api import Client
>
> with Client(<bam_host_url>) as client:
>     client.login(<username>, <password>)
>     tftp_deployment_role_id = client.add_tftp_deployment_role(
>         <tftp_group_id>, <server_id>
>     )
>     client.logout()
> print(tftp_deployment_role_id)
> ```

methods.name: bluecat_libraries.address_manager.api.client.Client.establish_trust_relationship
documentation:
Establish a trust relationship between a maximum of three Address Manager servers.
This is a prerequisite for configuring replication in Address Manager.

```
param remote_ip

The IP address of the standby server.

The standby server must be reachable from the primary server and must have
database access from the primary server. To enable database access, refer to the
Configuring database replication section in the .

param username

The username of an API user that logs in to Address Manager.

param password

The password of the specified API user.

param properties

This is reserved for future use.
```

Example:

> ```python
> from bluecat_libraries.address_manager.api import Client
>
> remote_ip = <ip_of_another_bam>
> username = <remote_bam_user>
> password = <remote_bam_password>
>
> with Client(<bam_host_url>) as client:
>     client.login(<username>, <password>)
>     client.establish_trust_relationship(remote_ip, username, password)
>     client.logout()
> ```

:::{versionadded} 21.11.1
:::
endregion TFTP
region Database Management

methods.name: bluecat_libraries.address_manager.api.client.Client.remove_trust_relationship
documentation:
Remove a remote Address Manager server from the trust relationship.

```

replication before it can be removed from the trust relationship.

> ```python

methods.name: bluecat_libraries.address_manager.api.client.Client.configure_streaming_replication
documentation:
Enable database replication on a remote system to automate the setup of replication between
two or three Address Manager systems.

:::{note}
The Address Manager server that this method runs against becomes the primary
Address Manager.
:::

```
param standby_server

The IP address of the standby Address Manager.

When adding a standby server, the server cannot be part of
an existing database replication environment or a removed standby server.
You can only add a standby server if it is operating as a Standalone server.

param compress_replication

If set to True, compress database replication files.
The default value is False.

Compressing database replication files is a resource-intensive process
that might affect system performance. Use caution when performing this action.

param latency_warning_threshold

The value to specify the warning threshold latency of replication,
in seconds (sec). Valid values for the parameter range from 0 to any positive value.
The default value is 3600 seconds.

param latency_critical_threshold

The value to specify the critical threshold latency of replication,
in seconds (sec). Valid values for the parameter range from 0 to any positive value.
The default value is 86400 seconds.

param properties

A dictionary containing the following option:

secondStandbyServer - Used to add an additional standby server. The server's IP address is required.
```

Example:

> ```python
> from bluecat_libraries.address_manager.api import Client
>
> standby_server = <ip4_address>
> properties = {"secondStandbyServer": <ip4_address>}
>
> with Client(<bam_host_url>) as client:
>     client.login(<username>, <password>)
>     client.configure_streaming_replication(standby_server, False, 3600, 86400, properties)
>     client.logout()
> ```

:::{versionadded} 21.11.1
:::

methods.name: bluecat_libraries.address_manager.api.client.Client.failover_replication
documentation:
Perform a manual replication failover.

```
param standby_server

The IP address of the standby server, which will become the primary
BAM server once a failover has been performed.

param properties

A dictionary containing the following option:

forceFailover - A boolean value indicates whether or not a forced failover.

If the latency of the database replication of the server relative to the
primary server is greater than 0, a forced failover can be performed.
```

Example:

> ```python
> from bluecat_libraries.address_manager.api import Client
>
> standby_server = <ip4_address>
> properties = {"forceFailover": "true"}
>
> with Client(<bam_host_url>) as client:
>     client.login(<username>, <password>)
>     client.failover_replication(standby_server, properties)
>     client.logout()
> ```

:::

methods.name: bluecat_libraries.address_manager.api.client.Client.purge_history_now
documentation:
Run the history purge function.

```
param number_of_days_to_keep

The number of days to preserve the data in
the database. Valid values for this parameter are between 1 and 3650. Set to -1
if using either until_when_timestamp or number_of_months_to_keep.
Using this parameter will archive all history that will be purged and overwrite existing
archive files in the /data/archive directory.

param number_of_months_to_keep

The number of months to preserve data
in the database. Valid values are integers of 0 or greater.
Set to -1 if using either until_when_timestamp or
number_of_days_to_keep. Using this parameter will not archive any data.

param until_when_timestamp

A string specifying a point in time after which history is
will be preserved. The valid timestamp pattern is YYYY-MM[-DD[ HH:MM:SS[.mmm]]]. Set this
parameter is None if using either number_of_days_to_keep or
number_of_months_to_keep. Using this parameter will not archive any data.

param wait_option

If set to True, the purge will be performed and
the result will be returned when completed. The default value is False.

return

Returns 0 (zero) when the purge service has successfully completed, otherwise -1.
```

Example:

> ```python
> from bluecat_libraries.address_manager.api import Client
>
> with Client(<bam_host_url>) as client:
>     client.login(<username>, <password>)
>     result = client.purge_history_now(2, -1)
>     client.logout()
> print(result)
> ```

:::{versionadded} 22.4.1
:::

methods.name: bluecat_libraries.address_manager.api.client.Client.create_xha_pair
documentation:
Create an xHA pair.

```
param configuration_id

The object ID of the configuration in which the xHA servers are
located.

param active_server_id

The object ID of the active DNS/DHCP Server.

param passive_server_id

The object ID of the passive DNS/DHCP Server.

param active_server_new_ip4_address

The new IPv4 address for the active server.

This is the physical interface of the active server used during creation of
the pair. The original IP address of the active server is assigned to
the virtual interface.

param properties

A dictionary containing the following options:

activeServerPassword: The deployment password for the active server.

passiveServerPassword: The deployment password for the passive server.

For more information on default server password, refer to BlueCat default
login credentials (the user must be authenticated to view this topic).

pingAddress: An IPv4 address that is accessible to both active and passive servers
in the xHA pair.

ip6Address: An optional IPv6 address for the xHA pair.

newManagementAddress: The new IPv4 address for the Management interface for
the active server (only for DNS/DHCP Servers with dedicated management enabled).

backboneActiveServerIPv4Address: The IPv4 address of the xHA interface for
the active server (eth1).

backboneActiveServerIPv4Netmask: The IPv4 netmask of the xHA interface for
the active server (eth1).

backbonePassiveServerIPv4Address: The IPv4 address of the xHA interface for
the passive server (eth1).

backbonePassiveServerIPv4Netmask: The IPv4 netmask of the xHA interface for
the passive server (eth1).

activeServerIPv4AddressForNAT: The inside virtual IPv4 address for
the active server.

passiveServerIPv4AddressForNAT: The inside virtual IPv4 address for
the passive server.

activeServerNewIPv4AddressForNAT: The inside physical IPv4 address for
the active server.
```

Example:

> ```python
> from bluecat_libraries.address_manager.api import Client
>
> properties = {"activeServerPassword": "bluecat", "passiveServerPassword": "bluecat"}
>
> with Client(<bam_host_url>) as client:
>     client.login(<username>, <password>)
>     client.create_xha_pair(
>         <configuration_id>, <active_server_id>, <passive_server_id>,
>         <active_server_new_ip4_address>, properties
>     )
>     client.logout()
> ```

:::{versionadded} 21.11.1
:::
endregion Migration

>     client.logout()

:::{versionadded} 21.11.1
:::

A client for calling BAM REST API v1 endpoints.

This client tracks authentication tokens and sends them with subsequent requests.

This client can be used as a context manager to automatically release used resources. Doing so
does not automatically call the `logout` method. If not used as a context manager, we
recommend you call the `close` method a single time.

```
param url

URL with a scheme that indicates how to connect to the BlueCat Address Manager.

param verify

If true (the default), BAM's TLS certificate is verified when using HTTPS
communications. If a string, it is treated as a path to the CA bundle to use.
This parameter mirrors the use of the parameter with the same name in the methods in the
requests Python library.
```

Example:

> ```python
> from bluecat_libraries.address_manager.api import Client
>
> with Client(<bam_host_url>) as client:
>     client.login(<username>, <password>)
>     data = client.get_system_info()
>     client.logout()
>
> for key, value in data.items():
>     print('{}: {}'.format(key, value))
> ```
>
> Sample protocol and certificate validation scenarios:
>
> ```python
> # Use HTTP
> Client("http://bam.example.com")
>
> # Use HTTPS and the certificate must be valid and trusted by the default CAs.
> Client("https://bam.example.com")
>
> # Use HTTPS, but ignore certificate verification errors. Not recommended, but useful
> # in case of self-signed certificates.
> Client("https://bam.example.com", verify=False)
>
> # Use HTTPS with the expectation that the certificate that BAM sends is either the same
> # as the one in the specified file, or signed by the CA certificate in it.
> # Typically, this scenario applies when using self-signed certificates.
> Client("https://bam.example.com", verify="/path/to/file")
>
> # Use HTTPS with the expectation that the certificate that BAM sends is either the same
> # as the one in the specified directory, or signed by a CA certificate in it.
> # The directory must have been processed with the ``c_rehash`` utility provided by OpenSSL.
> Client("https://bam.example.com", verify="/path/to/dir")
> ```

:::{versionadded} 21.5.1
:::

:::{versionchanged} 22.4.1 Use ``https`` as fallback if the passed BAM URL does not have a scheme. Previously, ``http`` was used.
:::
